headline: Crypto Weekly: Robinhood disappoints, Trump hosts meme coin winners
summary:
STORY: From falling shares at Robinhood to Trump hosting crypto contest winners... This is Crypto Weekly

:: Crypto Weekly

:: Robinhood shares fall

Robinhood's shares plunged 10%, as crypto-driven market volatility dampened its first-quarter trading volume growth....

leading the trading platform to miss estimates for profit and revenue.

Crypto markets have come under pressure this year...

with bitcoin down more than 30% in the last six months, as a broad risk-off mood took hold.

:: Cambodian senator's 'crypto-romance scams'

The U.S. government imposed sanctions on Kok An, a wealthy Cambodian senator and ally of Cambodian Senate President Hun Sen.

It said he, as well as 28 individuals and entities, had stolen millions of dollars from U.S. citizens using crypto-romance schemes.

The U.S. Treasury Department said Kok An used his political connections to protect a network of scam centers and various operators.

:: US sanctions 'wallets' tied to Iran 

The U.S. Treasury Department is sanctioning multiple wallets tied to Iran...

and freezing $344 million in cryptocurrency.

Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent announced the move in a statement on X.

It's part of an ongoing effort by the Trump administration to cut off funding sources to the Iranian government.

:: Trump hosts crypto contest winners

U.S. President Donald Trump hosted winners of his second annual meme coin contest at his Mar-a-Lago club in Palm Beach, Florida.

He offered top buyers of his $TRUMP cryptocurrency an audience with him...

even as the token's value has plunged more than 95% from its peak last year.

It comes as scrutiny of the Trump family’s broader crypto ventures has intensified, with Democratic leaders calling for investigations.

content.title: Crypto Weekly: Robinhood disappoints, Trump hosts meme coin winners

headline: The NYSE Is Going 24/7 – This Stock Could Surge by Triple Digits
summary: The NYSE filed for 24/7 tokenized trading in January 2026, and Robinhood (NASDAQ:HOOD) CEO Vlad Tenev has been asking for this since 2022. Robinhood is already the go-to platform for millions of investors and traders. Having the NYSE open to 24/7 trading instead of just during work hours, 5 days a week, would be a massive ... The NYSE Is Going 24/7 – This Stock Could Surge by Triple Digits
